_meta.schema: III ADR-003 §2 local learning store — ACCUMULATE writes target this file; canonical upstream (III.aDNA, md5 5adb0dfa38d9224649c3b2cba83852ae) is read-only from the consumer side
_meta.graduation: frequency>=3 across >=2 sessions + acceptance>=0.80 + Stanley/Argus gate (III ADR-003 §3)

_meta.summary: Producer input carries a human label per source (e.g. brief_consumer / document_generator sources[].label) but the source degrades to a bare URL `link` node in the .canvas (baseline JSON Canvas link nodes have no anchor-text slot), so provenance is present in-spec yet renders as a bare URL with no visible label.
_meta.proposed_fix: Producer-side: emit an adjacent caption/text node carrying the label, or fold the label into _reserved.component_types[node].qualities.label; OR a Standard-side errata defining a link-label carry in _reserved. Do not force a label into the baseline link node.
_meta.note: Local candidate (frequency 2 across 2 sessions, 2 producers). Recurred on document_generator (E4.1, 5 citation nodes); the >=2-sessions bar is now met but frequency<3, so not graduated. Feeds a generator/Standard errata pass.

_meta.summary: The document_generator (E4.1) lays content out as a single-pass deterministic vertical stack with fixed page boxes; it does not reflow or re-paginate, so a content-heavy page can overflow its page{p} bounds. Conformance is unaffected (geometry is positional and pages stay within doc_root), but visual fidelity needs real pagination.
_meta.proposed_fix: Add reflow/auto-pagination (measure content, break pages) as an E4.2 format-contract concern (length_window -> page breaks) and a PT-P5 render concern; E4.1 ships the fixed-stack skeleton deliberately.
_meta.note: Local candidate only (frequency 1). Producer-layout limitation surfaced by the first long-form consumer; not a Standard gap.
